mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS stays near the zero lower bound, the market prices prolonged accommodation and higher equity valuations; if short rates stop falling, the easing impulse fades and the S&P 500 tends to rise over the next month. A sustained increase in FEDFUNDS or a clear removal of easing would refute this link.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is flat or rising but 20-day SPX returns are still negative in the majority of such observations, or if the sign of the return relation flips for more than two consecutive years, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in UNRATE below its recent trend signals improving household income and hiring momentum, which supports risk appetite and produces positive 20-day SPX returns. The effect fails if unemployment falls while payroll growth and labor participation remain weak.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with falling UNRATE do not outperform unconditional 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is also rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal spending and revenue growth, which should support equities over a 20-day horizon through improved sales expectations and operating leverage. The signal fails when sales accelerate only because of temporary price inflation without real demand improvement.
failure_condition: If higher RSAFS growth does not correspond to a higher probability of positive 20-day SPX returns over rolling 3-year windows, or if the relation turns negative during multiple expansion phases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises from a depressed level, the improvement in industrial activity signals stronger real output and cyclical earnings support, which lifts equities over the next month. The effect should fail if industrial output improves only because of inventory restocking without end-demand strength.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO upturns are followed by negative or below-baseline 20-day SPX returns in most cases, or if HOUST does not confirm the activity rebound.

mechanism_narrative: A faster-than-expected CPIAUCSL acceleration raises inflation risk and pushes markets to price tighter policy, which pressures equities lower over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if higher CPI is offset by a simultaneous decline in FEDFUNDS expectations or if inflation is fully anticipated.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if CPI inflation surprises above trend do not lead to a higher share of negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign weakens materially when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST captures housing-led demand weakness and deteriorating cyclical confidence, which typically spills into equities with a lag of several weeks. The signal is strongest when homebuilding falls alongside weak retail sales.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from a 6-month high
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST drawdowns are not followed by negative 60-day-equivalent equity performance, or if the effect vanishes when RSAFS remains resilient.

mechanism_narrative: In a weak labor market with UNRATE still elevated and PAYEMS not improving, negative income expectations dominate and equities fall over the next month. If payroll growth turns positive at the same time, the bearish labor signal should disappear.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains above 7% and PAYEMS is flat or declining over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if high-unemployment, flat-payroll episodes do not predict below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive payroll momentum fully offsets elevated UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, stable or rising CPIAUCSL combined with unchanged FEDFUNDS implies real policy remains too loose relative to inflation, which eventually lifts term premiums and supports a 10-year yield rise over the next month, pressuring equities lower. The effect fails once inflation decelerates or policy tightening accelerates.
failure_condition: Reject if, in tightening cycles, flat-to-rising CPI with unchanged FEDFUNDS does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the relation reverses when inflation decelerates.
